title: Kidney Now!

jokes.text: Of course, I was a kidney in my fifth grade school play.
jokes.setup: Jack asks if Liz knows what a kidney does
jokes.punchline: Liz's oddly specific and useless personal anecdote
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - deflecting serious conversation with irrelevant personal detail. Sets up musical callback.

jokes.text: # Oh, I'm the kidney... # The other kidney is singing now.
jokes.setup: Liz mentions being a kidney in a school play
jokes.punchline: She actually starts singing the kidney song, then acknowledges the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity by actually performing, then meta-commentary makes it even funnier. Great commitment to the bit.

jokes.text: Milton needs a kidney. Milton, as in your dad? My dad? I don't know this guy.
jokes.setup: Liz assumes Milton is Jack's father
jokes.punchline: Jack's emotionally distant denial of paternal connection
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack emotional avoidance. The casual dismissal is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And since Giuliani left, it's gotten tougher to harvest hobo organs.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining difficulty of organ procurement
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to harvesting homeless people's organs under previous mayor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - treats horrific concept as minor bureaucratic inconvenience. Perfect Jack corporate sociopathy.

jokes.text: # The brain helps you make decisions... #
jokes.setup: Jack needs help with difficult decision
jokes.punchline: Liz starts singing educational organ song instead of helping
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Liz's unhelpful nature. The educational song format is amusing.

jokes.text: Give me my money, you bitch! Never! [screaming]
jokes.setup: Jenna recounting previous Vontella Show appearance
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was trashy daytime TV fight with alleged half-sister
jokes.explanation: Good setup for trashy talk show environment. Shows Jenna's willingness to do anything for attention.

jokes.text: And I was all like, 'talk to the hand.' You give me back my man, bitch! Never!
jokes.setup: Liz remembering another trashy Vontella appearance
jokes.punchline: Liz was also on the show fighting over a man
jokes.explanation: Funny that both characters have degrading talk show history. 'Talk to the hand' dates the reference nicely.

jokes.text: [Laughs] 'Kidney' is just such a funny word.
jokes.setup: Leo apologizes for laughing during serious medical discussion
jokes.punchline: Claims it's just the word 'kidney' that's making him laugh
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman character moment - inappropriate behavior with pseudo-medical justification.

jokes.text: This is incorrect, doctor. I'm not giving him a kidney. It's the other way around.
jokes.setup: Medical form confusion
jokes.punchline: Milton thinks Jack is giving HIM the kidney
jokes.explanation: Good setup for medical incompetence running gag. Reasonable confusion joke.

jokes.text: No, I'll remember. Opposite, opposite, opposite.
jokes.setup: Leo refusing to rewrite the incorrect forms
jokes.punchline: His solution is to chant 'opposite' to remember
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of medical incompetence. The chanting makes it more absurd.

jokes.text: I think it's the hard 'K' sound that's making me giggle. Kidney.
jokes.setup: Leo continuing to laugh inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Now claims it's specifically the 'K' sound, then says 'kidney' again
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the absurdity. Testing his own theory makes it funnier.

jokes.text: And they said you can drop up to five 'f-bombs.'
jokes.setup: Kenneth telling Tracy about graduation speaking opportunity
jokes.punchline: High school has specific profanity allowance for Tracy
jokes.explanation: Funny that they know Tracy well enough to pre-negotiate profanity limits. Good character specificity.

jokes.text: Because Frank Lucas High School was a hellhole. A drug dealer named Campbell, he ruled that school.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he won't go back to his high school
jokes.punchline: Sets up dramatic gangster backstory
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the Campbell reveal. Creates false dramatic tension.

jokes.text: Campbell was actually Mr. Campbell, his science teacher. His science teacher was a drug dealer?
jokes.setup: Tracy's dramatic story about dangerous Campbell
jokes.punchline: Reveals Campbell was just his science teacher
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. The mundane reality vs. Tracy's dramatic perception is perfect.

jokes.text: Science was my most favorite subject especially the Old Testament.
jokes.setup: Tracy defending his love of science
jokes.punchline: Confuses the Bible with science curriculum
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy confusion. Shows his educational gaps in a funny way.

jokes.text: Mr. Campbell wanted him to dissect a frog. Tracy couldn't do it. He cried in front of the whole class.
jokes.setup: Built up story about Campbell wanting Tracy to 'cut up Baby'
jokes.punchline: Was just standard frog dissection, Tracy was too sensitive
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Tracy's tough guy image. The mundane truth is much funnier than the myth.

jokes.text: Just like Peter did to Jesus in Science.
jokes.setup: Grizz comparing Tracy's shame to biblical betrayal
jokes.punchline: Says it happened 'in Science' class instead of history
jokes.explanation: Funny confusion of Bible stories with science class. Good character moment for Grizz's earnest confusion.

jokes.text: That actually happened to Liz. Vontella don't care who Liz is.
jokes.setup: Jenna sharing that the dealbreaker scenario happened to Liz
jokes.punchline: Vontella's complete dismissal of who Liz is
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Vontella's blunt dismissiveness. Jenna's oversharing backfires perfectly.

jokes.text: You have sexually-transmitted crazy mouth. Dealbreaker.
jokes.setup: Complex tanning salon relationship drama
jokes.punchline: Liz diagnoses the convoluted explanation as a symptom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant coinage. 'Sexually-transmitted crazy mouth' is inspired wordplay that perfectly captures the absurdity.

jokes.text: Nope, your fiancé's gay. Look at him. Look at you. Classic case of 'fruit blindness.'
jokes.setup: Woman asking about wedding planning arguments
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately diagnoses homosexuality and names the condition
jokes.explanation: 'Fruit blindness' is a clever coinage for an obvious relationship dynamic. Good rapid-fire diagnosis.

jokes.text: Sir, have you ever kissed a gentleman? Uh... one time in college. And a lot since then.
jokes.setup: Liz pressing the fiancé about his sexuality
jokes.punchline: His answer escalates from experimental to frequent
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure. The casual admission makes Liz's diagnosis even more accurate.

jokes.text: Yeah, he thinks he deserves a 'va-jay-jay upgrade.' He doesn't, he's not Tom Brady.
jokes.setup: Woman complaining about boyfriend's changed behavior
jokes.punchline: Specific diagnosis with celebrity comparison for worthiness
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective. Tom Brady as the standard for deserving upgrades is a good cultural reference.

jokes.text: Yeah, there's no such thing as bisexual. That's just something they invented in the '90s to sell hair products.
jokes.setup: General relationship advice context
jokes.punchline: Absurd conspiracy theory about bisexuality's commercial origins
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd conspiracy theory. The specificity of '90s hair products makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Only one snake in the bed. Dealbreaker.
jokes.setup: Rapid-fire dealbreaker advice
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary and oddly specific snake-related relationship rule
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity. Implies multiple snakes would be acceptable, which is funnier.

jokes.text: It's true! There is no Baby! I was chicken! I was chicken!
jokes.setup: Kenneth confronting Tracy about the truth
jokes.punchline: Tracy's emotional breakdown confessing his sensitivity
jokes.explanation: Good emotional payoff. The repetition of 'I was chicken' while crying is perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: A guy crying about a chicken and a baby? I thought this was a comedy show.
jokes.setup: Tracy's emotional confession
jokes.punchline: Audience member breaks fourth wall commenting on genre confusion
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment. Random audience member commenting on the show's tonal shift is unexpected and funny.

jokes.text: [Crying]: Don't die! I love you, Jack! [various crying clips]
jokes.setup: Tracy claims he never cries
jokes.punchline: Montage of him crying multiple times throughout the series
jokes.explanation: Excellent use of series history. Multiple examples prove he's lying to himself. Great visual comedy.

jokes.text: I have a wolf dog, I have two bad knees and a gun... that I lost.
jokes.setup: Tracy listing evidence of his badass adult status
jokes.punchline: Deflating admission that he lost the gun
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation. Builds up tough image then immediately undercuts it. Good character work.

jokes.text: We let things fester until they erupt in inappropriate anger preferably during a wedding or elementary school graduation.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why Donaghys don't talk about feelings
jokes.punchline: Specific examples of inappropriate venues for emotional outbursts
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight. The specificity of venues and 'preferably' makes it perfect Jack behavior.

jokes.text: I have great seats in the section between the players' wives and players' mistresses. But I don't go on Bat Day.
jokes.setup: Jack offering to take Milton to Yankees game
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions privileged seating arrangement and arbitrary restriction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack elite access casual mention. The Bat Day avoidance is unexplained and funnier for it.

jokes.text: No, that's a ball he fouled off in the third inning. Huh.
jokes.setup: Milton asks about Carlton Fisk home run ball
jokes.punchline: It's actually a much less significant foul ball, Jack's underwhelmed reaction
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of memorabilia excitement. Jack's casual 'huh' shows his disappointment subtly.

jokes.text: No, that's a ball he fouled off in the third inning.
jokes.setup: Milton asks about Carlton Fisk's famous home run ball
jokes.punchline: It's just a random foul ball, not the historic one
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that deflates expectations about sports memorabilia

jokes.text: We called him Mean Steve, but his real name was Steven Killer.
jokes.setup: Tracy pointing out a former bad classmate
jokes.punchline: The 'real name' is even more intimidating than the nickname
jokes.explanation: Good subversion. Usually nicknames are tougher than real names. 'Steven Killer' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Because you're not a bad-bleep O.G. You're a sensitive artist.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining how Tracy escaped street life
jokes.punchline: Kenneth using bleeped street terminology while being wholesome
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast. Kenneth using street slang while maintaining his innocent demeanor is perfect.

jokes.text: I was really looking forward to putting your father's kidney in you. The other way around, Leo.
jokes.setup: Leo discussing the failed kidney match
jokes.punchline: Still has the donor/recipient backwards despite all the previous confusion
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the medical incompetence. Leo never learning shows consistency in his character.

jokes.text: The same manipulation machine that got people to vote for Barack Obama and donate all that money after Rainstorm Katrina.
jokes.setup: Jack planning to use liberal media for kidney campaign
jokes.punchline: Calls Hurricane Katrina 'Rainstorm Katrina' and frames Obama election as manipulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack conservative perspective. 'Rainstorm Katrina' minimizes the disaster in character.

jokes.text: Like 'We Are the World' or Weird Al Yankovic's less successful parody benefit, 'We Are the Pizza.'
jokes.setup: Liz suggesting celebrity benefit concert
jokes.punchline: Fictional Weird Al parody that failed
jokes.explanation: 'We Are the Pizza' is perfectly absurd Weird Al parody. The failure of a charity parody is darkly funny.

jokes.text: I haven't seen that many riled-up dirt bags since CVS put the cold medicine behind the counter.
jokes.setup: Jack commenting on Vontella Show audience reaction
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to meth cook ingredient restrictions
jokes.explanation: Good topical reference that paints both audiences as problematic. Specific and observant.

jokes.text: Suburban seventh graders have more sexual experience than you do but that doesn't matter.
jokes.setup: Liz questioning her qualification to give relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Jack's brutal assessment of her inexperience
jokes.explanation: Brutal and specific insult that's perfectly in character for Jack's dismissiveness about Liz's personal life.

jokes.text: [Laughs] What do you think this is, Wings?
jokes.setup: Liz thinks T.G.S. has 20 years left
jokes.punchline: Reference to long-running NBC sitcom as comparison
jokes.explanation: Good meta reference to network television realities. Wings as a longevity comparison is specific and apt.

jokes.text: My Mary J. Blige Foundation is celebrating its 10th year of searching for the Loch Ness Monster.
jokes.setup: Mary describing her charitable work
jokes.punchline: Her foundation searches for cryptozoological creatures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist charity concept. The formality of '10th year' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Well, musicians have banded together before to solve all kinds of problems: world hunger, the collapse of the American farm, global warming... and, uh... you're 0 for 3, guys.
jokes.setup: Clay listing previous celebrity charitable efforts
jokes.punchline: Jack points out their complete failure rate
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of celebrity activism. The matter-of-fact failure assessment is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Elvis, haven't you said that if a song reaches just one person, you've done your job? No, I've never said that.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to convince Elvis with inspiring quote
jokes.punchline: Elvis flat denial of ever saying anything inspirational
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of generic inspirational musician rhetoric. Elvis's blunt denial is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Who got you out of a 20-year exclusive performance contract at SeaWorld?
jokes.setup: Jack calling in favors from Mary J. Blige
jokes.punchline: Implies she was contractually obligated to perform at SeaWorld for decades
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept. Mary J. Blige trapped performing for marine mammals is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: And Elvis, or should I say, Declan McManus... international art thief...
jokes.setup: Jack leveraging Elvis Costello
jokes.punchline: Reveals his real name connects to art theft
jokes.explanation: Good use of Elvis Costello's real name for blackmail material. Art theft is unexpected criminal activity.

jokes.text: And I'm here in New York while he's back in Hooglanderveen. Long distance is the wrong distance, Sue.
jokes.setup: Paula explaining her relationship problem
jokes.punchline: Liz's rhyming dealbreaker advice and calling her the wrong name
jokes.explanation: Decent rhyming advice format. Getting the name wrong adds to the rushed TV expert persona.

jokes.text: Not on my watch, biotch. / 'S' that 'D.' Shut it down. Dealbreaker.
jokes.setup: Angie mentions Tracy's secret credit card
jokes.punchline: Liz's awkward attempt at urban slang
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz trying to adapt to her audience

jokes.text: No to the way to the Jose. / Long distance is the wrong distance, Sue. Dealbreaker.
jokes.setup: Liz continues giving advice to wives
jokes.punchline: Increasingly strained catchphrase attempts
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's desperation to create catchphrases

jokes.text: Cerie, I have two words for you: robot warning. Okay, that catch-phrase needs a little work. Dealbreaker!
jokes.setup: Liz trying out new catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Immediately recognizes it doesn't work and falls back on dealbreaker
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness moment. Shows Liz trying to build her brand but failing at catchphrase creation.

jokes.text: And I guarantee you, every single person in this room will one day be President of the United States.
jokes.setup: Tracy giving inspirational graduation speech
jokes.punchline: Impossible promise that everyone will be president
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - genuine inspiration undermined by mathematical impossibility. Sweetly absurd.

jokes.text: Okay, but I'm allergic to horses.
jokes.setup: Principal mentions a surprise
jokes.punchline: Tracy's random specific concern
jokes.explanation: Random Tracy fear that makes no contextual sense

jokes.text: Okay, but I'm allergic to horses.
jokes.setup: Being told there's a surprise
jokes.punchline: Random specific fear
jokes.explanation: Decent random character detail but not exceptional

jokes.text: I actually played a kidney in my fifth grade school play... with this loser.
jokes.setup: Liz relating to the kidney benefit
jokes.punchline: Reveals she and Jack were in the same school play, insults him casually
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds history between characters. Casual insult is typical Liz-Jack dynamic.

jokes.text: Do you know if there's a sit-down Quizno's in Midtown? Never mind.
jokes.setup: Liz excited about book agent lunch anywhere she wants
jokes.punchline: Her aspirational restaurant choice is fast food with seating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment. Even with unlimited choice, she aims for modest fast-casual upgrade.

jokes.text: It's a working farm and I'm the only one whose hands are big enough to guide the bull during mating.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining why he won't go on family vacation
jokes.punchline: Horrifying specific agricultural sexual duty
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and disturbing. The practical necessity makes it funnier and more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: You know why I get a hotel room? To poop in peace. No kids banging on the door. No phones ringing. It's my time... every Tuesday and Thursday at 3:00 P.M.!
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his secret hotel room
jokes.punchline: Elaborate bathroom privacy schedule with specific timing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from suspicious to mundane but elaborate. The specific schedule makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I don't know why I only go twice a week. That's what Angie should be worried about!
jokes.setup: Tracy's bathroom schedule revelation
jokes.punchline: Realizes his bowel movement frequency is the real concern
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shifts the concern to health issue. Tracy's self-awareness adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I only have $300 million.
jokes.setup: Liz worrying about financial security after T.G.S. ends
jokes.punchline: Treats massive wealth as inadequate
jokes.explanation: Good wealth perspective joke. Shows how disconnected from normal financial concerns the characters are.

jokes.text: [Thinking]: And so began the craziest summer of my life. How'd you say that without moving your mouth?
jokes.setup: Tracy having internal monologue moment
jokes.punchline: Kenneth somehow hears his thoughts and asks about the technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment breaking internal monologue convention. Kenneth's supernatural ability is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I have this weird loose feeling in my shoulders. What is that? It's either happiness or osteoporosis.
jokes.setup: Liz describing unfamiliar positive physical sensation
jokes.punchline: Can't distinguish happiness from bone disease
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment. Her unfamiliarity with happiness vs. health paranoia is spot-on.

jokes.text: We were best friends in elementary school.
jokes.setup: Liz gets excited about seeing Sheryl Crow
jokes.punchline: Reveals personal connection
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Liz's past connections

jokes.text: Milton Greene needs a kidney, just like I need this beard. You don't want to know what's under here.
jokes.setup: Elvis making comparison to establish necessity
jokes.punchline: Implies his face is horrifying under the beard
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating celebrity humor. The ominous warning about his face is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Think of it this way: if I had $2, I'd give you one. Wouldn't I? I'm one of the drunk ones.
jokes.setup: Clay making financial analogy for kidney donation
jokes.punchline: Admits he's intoxicated, undermining his logic
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier drunkenness admission. Self-awareness about impaired judgment adds humor.

jokes.text: If you had two heads, you'd wish you had one / If you had two dogs attacking you, you'd want just one / There, we proved a point
jokes.setup: Elvis tries to make logical argument for kidney donation
jokes.punchline: Completely illogical analogies declared as proof
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that makes no sense but is declared proven

jokes.text: A hand would be an even harder thing to give. If you can give a kidney...
jokes.setup: Song clarifying what Milton doesn't need
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging hand donation would be more difficult
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist logic in the charity song. The practical assessment of body part donation difficulty is morbidly funny.

jokes.text: What are you talking about? It's May. Shh.
jokes.setup: Singer says they've had quite a year
jokes.punchline: Other singer points out it's only May, gets shushed
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment about charity song clichés. Breaking the fourth wall during performance adds humor.

tmdb_overview: Jack's biological father's health hangs in the balance as the pair start to build a relationship. Liz draws some attention as a relationship expert, despite her own many failed relationships, after an appearance on a talk show. Tracy is invited to speak at his old high school and Kenneth helps him overcome some embarrassing memories from those years.
insight: Kidney telethon format lets 66 jokes rip at 2.55-per-minute clip—Season 3's densest finale.
